details.body: finding_summary: `ProcessBackendAdapter` and `TmuxAdapter` still pass an empty stdout string into `classify_exit_failure_with_output()` whenever `extract_stdout_error()` returns `None`, so backends that print exhaustion text only to plain stdout are still classified as `TransportFailure` and retried instead of degrading. severity: high affected_files: ["src/adapters/process_backend.rs:1562-1585", "src/adapters/process_backend.rs:1626-1638", "src/adapters/tmux.rs:632-646"] remediation_hint: When there is no structured stdout error, pass a bounded plain-text stdout tail into the classifier in both the normal path and the retry-without-resume path.

finding_summary: The exhaustion matcher still misses common plain-text quota wording such as `insufficient credits`; `BACKEND_EXHAUSTED_PATTERNS` only matches narrower phrases like `credits exhausted`, `credits depleted`, `purchase more credits`, and `insufficient_quota`. severity: medium affected_files: ["src/adapters/process_backend.rs:1942-1952"] remediation_hint: Broaden the plain-text exhaustion patterns used for stdout/stderr classification to cover unstructured credit-insufficiency wording without weakening the existing transient-rate-limit safeguards.

finding_summary: Regression coverage for stdout-only exhaustion classification is still missing. The process-backend tests cover structured stdout JSON and stderr fallback, but not a plain-text stdout-only exhaustion case, and the tmux adapter tests contain no failure-classification coverage for this path. severity: medium affected_files: ["tests/unit/process_backend_test.rs:2131-2200", "tests/unit/tmux_adapter_test.rs:1-626"] remediation_hint: Add adapter-level tests where stderr is empty and stdout contains a plain-text exhaustion message, then assert `BackendExhausted` so graceful degradation avoids retries.

The core implementation for the bead is completely missing. No source files have been modified (verified via git diff and git status). Both call sites — process_backend.rs:1633 and tmux.rs:638 — still use `stdout_error.as_deref().unwrap_or_default()` which falls back to an empty string when `extract_stdout_error()` returns `None`. This means stdout-only exhaustion messages (e.g., "You've hit your usage limit" printed to stdout without a structured JSON envelope) are still classified as TransportFailure instead of BackendExhausted.

All five acceptance criteria are unmet:
1. Backends printing exhaustion messages only to stdout are NOT classified as BackendExhausted
2. No new graceful degradation path for stdout-only messages
3. (N/A — no changes to break stderr-based classification)
4. No regression test for stdout-only exhaustion path
5. No code changes to pass cargo test/clippy/fmt checks against

Remediation: In both process_backend.rs (around line 1633) and tmux.rs (around line 638), when `extract_stdout_error()` returns `None`, pass a bounded tail of the raw stdout text (e.g., via `truncate_str_tail(&stdout_text, STDERR_EXHAUSTION_SCAN_LIMIT)`) as the stdout parameter to `classify_exit_failure_with_output`. Update the `classify_exit_failure_with_output` docstring to reflect this new fallback behavior. Add a regression test that constructs a non-zero exit with a plain-text stdout containing an exhaustion keyword (no JSON envelope) and verifies classification as BackendExhausted.

Critical path still misses plain-stdout exhaustion detection. In `src/adapters/process_backend.rs`, both non-zero-exit paths pass `stdout_for_class` as `stdout_error.as_deref().unwrap_or_default()` (lines `~1562`, `~1575`, and `~1633`), and `src/adapters/tmux.rs` does the same at lines `~632-642`. If `extract_stdout_error()` fails (plain text on stdout, not JSON), `classify_exit_failure_with_output()` receives `stdout=""` and backends that emit `usage limit`, `quota exceeded`, etc. only to stdout are classified as `TransportFailure` instead of `BackendExhausted` and retried. Add bounded raw stdout tail fallback (similar to `STDERR_EXHAUSTION_SCAN_LIMIT`) for classification when `extract_stdout_error` is `None`, and pass that to `classify_exit_failure_with_output()`.
